title: History, Objects, References, and Forensic Inspection · Recovery Case: Show Cat File
summary: Explain when and why a behavior changed using graph, path, object, author, and content evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git show cat file increment in a history-forensics evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect commits, trees, blobs, tags, and object types without checking out or executing repository content.
Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee3; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. A defensible solution applies Show Cat File, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a history-forensics evidence packet.

content.code:
git log --graph --decorate --oneline --all
git show --stat --summary HEAD
git cat-file -t HEAD^{tree}
git log --follow -- docs/notes.txt
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: git show always displays a text patch regardless of object type. This fails the Show Cat File contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
